risk_if_not_implemented: Without Obligation To Inform Third-Parties, third-party risks may go unmanaged, creating supply-chain exposure that compromises the organization.

compensating_control_1.name: Information Sharing With Third Parties
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to disclose Personal Data (PD) to third-parties only for the purposes identified in the data privacy notice and with the implicit or explicit consent of the data subject.
compensating_control_1.justification: Information Sharing With Third Parties (PRI-07) provides third-party oversight that compensates for the absence of Obligation To Inform Third-Parties (PRI-07.3) by extending security obligations contractually and monitoring third-party risk in lieu of direct control. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Information Sharing
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to utilize a process to assist users in making information sharing decisions to ensure data is appropriately protected.
compensating_control_2.justification: Information Sharing (DCH-14) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Obligation To Inform Third-Parties (PRI-07.3) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Process applicability. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
